original_question:
Patrick started walking at a constant rate along a straight road from school to the park. One hour after Patrick left, Tanya started running along the same road from school to the park. One hour after Tanya left, Jose started bicycling along the same road from school to the park. Tanya ran at a constant rate of $2$ miles per hour faster than Patrick walked, Jose bicycled at a constant rate of $7$ miles per hour faster than Tanya ran, and all three arrived at the park at the same time. The distance from the school to the park is $\frac{m}{n}$ miles, where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m + n$.

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: Emma started walking at a constant rate along a straight road from school to the park. One hour after Emma left, Liam started running along the same road from school to the park. One hour after Liam left, Noah started bicycling along the same road from school to the park. Liam ran at a constant rate of $3$ miles per hour faster than Emma walked, Noah bicycled at a constant rate of $8$ miles per hour faster than Liam ran, and all three arrived at the park at the same time. The distance from the school to the park is $\frac{m}{n}$ miles, where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m + n$.

Sophia started walking at a constant rate along a straight road from school to the park. One hour after Sophia left, Jake started running along the same road from school to the park. One hour after Jake left, Mia started bicycling along the same road from school to the park. Jake ran at a constant rate of $4$ miles per hour faster than Sophia walked, Mia bicycled at a constant rate of $6$ miles per hour faster than Jake ran, and all three arrived at the park at the same time. The distance from the school to the park is $\frac{m}{n}$ miles, where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m + n$.

Ethan ran at a constant rate of $5$ miles per hour faster than Olivia walked, Ava bicycled at a constant rate of $9$ miles per hour faster than Ethan ran, and all three arrived at the park at the same time. The distance from the school to the park is $\frac{m}{n}$ miles, where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m + n$.

The distance from the school to the park is $\frac{m}{n}$ miles, where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m + n$.

One hour after Chloe left, Liam started bicycling along the same road from school to the park. Chloe ran at a constant rate of $2$ miles per hour faster than Aiden walked, Liam bicycled at a constant rate of $10$ miles per hour faster than Chloe ran, and all three arrived at the park at the same time.
